channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Honduras holds primaries as voter frustration simmers over security and the economy
item.description: TEGUCIGALPA, Honduras (AP) — Voters in Honduras will select candidates from the three main parties Sunday to compete in November’s general election for the presidency in a country that remains deeply polarized, but skeptical of leaders from the left and right who have failed to deliver on improving
item.dc:creator: MARLON GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: China learned from Trump's first trade war and changed its tactics when tariffs came again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The leaders of both Canada and Mexico got on the phone with President Donald Trump this past week to seek solutions after he slapped tariffs on their countries, but China’s president appears unlikely to make a similar call soon.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh brace for upcoming food reductions as aid agencies cut funding
item.description: COX’S BAZAR, Bangladesh (AP) — Rohingya refugees in crammed Bangladeshi camps say they are worried about a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SHAFIQUR RAHMAN and JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 days of clashes and revenge killings in Syria leave more than 1,000 people dead
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The death toll from two days of clashes between Syrian security forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad and revenge killings that followed has risen to more than 1,000, a war monitoring group said Saturday, making it one of the deadliest acts of violence since Syria’s con
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 8 hurt when driver plows vehicle into Southern California car dealership
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Eight people were injured Saturday when a driver plowed a vehicle into a CarMax location in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Wall Street Journal: 'Someone should sue' Trump over tariffs
item.description: In a recent op-ed, the board warned of a possible jolt in consumer prices once the tariffs go into full effect and urged someone to file a lawsuit in response.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans unveiled a spending bill Saturday that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30, pushing ahead with a go-it-alone strategy that seems certain to spark a major confrontation with Democrats over the contours of government spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge awards nearly $2M after concluding FBI agent was negligent in shooting kidnapped Texas man
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge has awarded nearly $2 million in damages as part of a civil lawsuit after concluding an FBI agent was negligent when he fatally shot a kidnapped Texas man during a botched rescue attempt in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, KRON4

item.title: Total lunar eclipse, blood moon will be visible in March
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy arrested for assault in Texas
item.description: On Friday, Chiefs star rookie Xavier Worthy, 21, was arrested in Williamson County, Texas after investigators said he assaulted a family member or person in his household by "impeding their breathing or circulation.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail to return to theaters
item.description: The movie, which first hit theaters in 1975, will return to the big screen for a limited time in celebration of its 50th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Space Force's mysterious X-37B plane returns to Earth after 434 days in orbit
item.description: The secretive Space Force plane X-37B touched down to earth on Friday after spending 434 days in orbit conducting mysterious tests.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Colombian priest, child molester sentenced for passport fraud and stripped of US citizenship
item.description: A former Colombian priest convicted of child molestation has been ordered deported back to his country and stripped of his U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine supporters focus on hope and resilience as US relations sour and war carries on
item.description: The theme of “hope” was chosen long before this year’s deterioration of Washington-Kyiv relations, but participants at an international Ukrainian studies conference said that hope is needed more than ever — not only in Ukraine but in the United States itself.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CEO of Jack Daniel's responds after whiskey pulled from Canada's shelves
item.description: The Distilled Spirits Council of the United States applauded President Donald Trump’s decision to suspend tariffs one day after some Canadian provinces started pulling U.S. liquor, including Jack Daniel's, from shelves.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walgreens set to go private in deal worth nearly $10 billion
item.description: Walgreens Boots Alliance says it has agreed to be acquired by private equity firm Sycamore Partners as the struggling retailer looks to turn itself around after years of losing money.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alarming number of Americans scammed out of life savings have one thing in common, prompting lawmaker response
item.description: With romance scams on the rise, a bipartisan group of lawmakers is introducing new legislation to hold fraudsters accountable for targeting Americans' savings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First national analysis finds America's butterflies are disappearing at 'catastrophic' rate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America's butterflies are disappearing because of insecticides, climate change and habitat loss, with the number of the winged beauties down 22% since 2000, a new study finds.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire leads police to body found in clothing donation bin
item.description: Police are investigating a body found in a clothing donation bin in the parking lot of 70 Hudson Avenue. According to the Glens Falls Police Department, around 2:21 a.m., officers responded to an active fire at the donation bin and discovered the deceased person inside.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Abel, WFLA, NBC
